forecast.name: Tonight
forecast.detailedForecast: Clear, with a low around 13. Southwest wind around 7 mph.

forecast.name: Tuesday
forecast.detailedForecast: Snow between 11am and 5pm, then rain and snow. Partly sunny, with a high near 34. South wind 6 to 9 mph. Chance of precipitation is 100%. New snow accumulation of less than one inch possible.

weather_report:
As the night deepens over Boston, the sky maintains its clear cloak, offering a crispness that beckons the adventurous spirit. With temperatures dipping to around 13 degrees, the air is unyielding and invigorating, complemented by a gentle southwest breeze that whispers through the streets. The shimmering lights of the city reflect a tranquil urgency, casting a soft glow that contrasts against the dark expanse of the night. There’s an undertone of anticipation for the snow that will soon lace the landscape, a promise of winter’s artistry set to unfold tomorrow.

As dawn breaks on Tuesday, a transformation will begin, with snow gracing the city between the late morning and early afternoon, unfurling into a delicate mix of rain and snow. While the high will reach merely 34 degrees, the inescapable chill will remain. It might be wise to don layers for warmth—maybe a cozy sweater under a sturdy coat, along with a knitted scarf and gloves to fend off any biting gusts. A prepared spirit could take advantage of this whimsical weather; a leisurely stroll through a snow-dusted park or perhaps finding refuge in a cozy café, enjoying fragrant coffee as the world outside transforms into a picturesque winter scene. While the rain and snow combine, the inner warmth of connection with the city and oneself may just turn an otherwise cold day into a cherished memory.
